title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bobbie recounts to Moira Nicholson the details regarding a photograph taken by Roger Baskin-Fontaine, suggesting Roger's involvement in the death of Allen Castles. Moira expresses disbelief that Roger would push Castles off a cliff, noting that the two men had only met once at lunch and lacked a motive. Distressed by the conversation and Bobbie's request to share her fears with Frankie, Moira flees the scene. Frankie follows her to provide comfort and arranges for the three of them to meet again in the future.

Returning to Bobbie, Frankie notes two significant observations: she previously saw Moira holding Sylvia Baskin-French's hand followed by a glare from Moira, and she realizes that Castles likely identified Moira from the photograph, which explains how he located her. Frankie and Bobbie speculate whether Moira killed Castles due to a personal motive involving Sylvia or a connection to a drug cartel. Frankie decides to observe Roger closely to see if he can explain the photograph; she considers that if he is honest, he might become an ally in protecting Henry Baskin-French from being sent to Grange. The pair also reflects on the mysterious and seemingly irrelevant role of a person named Evans.

Later, Frankie encounters Roger near the house. Roger informs her that Moira told Sylvia the truth about Henry and suggests that Henry requires treatment for drug addiction at Grange. When Frankie asks Roger directly if he ever took a photo from the pocket of the man who fell off the cliff, Roger reacts with slight annoyance and embarrassment but shows no signs of guilt or fear.
